---
title: "About Me!"
image: "miriam2025organChCh.jpeg"
about:
  template: trestles
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/miriamheiss
    - icon: instagram
      text: Instagram
      href: https://www.instagram.com/heiss.miriam/?hl=en
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:memerheiss@gmail.com

---

Hello there! 

I'm Miriam Heiss! I'm 16 years old and live in Georgia. I'm homeschooled and attending Brigham Young University Idaho as a concurrent enrollment student. I'm also a member of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints. I enjoy crocheting, sewing, knitting, reading and general craft-y things. I also play the organ (which you can see on my YouTube channel), and I have played the organ since 2018. When I have time, I compose and arrange as well. 

I began my organ journey when I was 8 years old at Brigham Young University, participating in the ["Organ First!"](https://organ.byu.edu/organ-first/) program, and then started private lessons when I moved to Georgia. I've played for my local LDS congregation since early 2022. While I haven't substituted or played for any other congregation, I have had experience playing many different kinds of organs at various churches—most notably a Flentrop tracker-action at Rivercliff Lutheran, a Rieger tracker-action at Christ Church cathedral in Oxford, and the Aeolian-Skinner at Johns Creek UMC.
